reasoning: The current threshold requires 11 "Strategic developments" in a 30-day period. Over the past year (Oct 2024 to Oct 2025), there was an average of only exactly 1 event per 360-day period recorded in New Caledonia. Achieving 11 such events within 30 days would demand an unprecedented mass outbreak similar to the heights of the May 2024 crisis, specifically driven by widespread looting, high-level arrests, or continuous daily peace agreements. As of late October 2025, the situation remains politically tense but broadly calm, following an agreement in July (the Bougival Accord) which cooled immediate violence. While provincial elections potentially scheduled for November 2025 pose a risk of reigniting unrest, the empirical base rate for a 10x spike of this nature is just 0.002. Given the short time passed from the cutoff for the first resolution date, and adhering to the minimum forecasting bound of 0.05, the predictions are anchored near the floor but with a slight upward tilt for the dates farther in the future to reflect increasing uncertainty regarding the November election aftermath.
